name: Employee Resources
description_short: Employee Resources Credit Union is a community-chartered credit union serving Lawrence, Giles, Wayne, Henderson, and McNairy counties in Tennessee with competitive loan rates and member-focused services.
description_long:
Employee Resources Credit Union (ERCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union operating in Tennessee with a focus on community-based financial services. The institution serves residents and workers in Lawrence, Giles, Wayne, Henderson, and McNairy counties, maintaining a local presence while offering modern digital banking capabilities.

ERCU provides a comprehensive suite of financial products including share accounts, auto loans (currently promoting rates as low as 3.99%), in-house real estate loans (rates from 5.50%), and various deposit products. The credit union offers online banking, mobile app access, EMV chip cards with instant issue capability, debit and credit cards, and flexible payment options through their RISE Payments system. Members can access calculators and branch information for financial planning.

What distinguishes ERCU is its unique lifetime membership promise—"Once a Member, Always a Member"—meaning membership persists even after job changes, retirement, or relocation, requiring only a $5 minimum balance and one annual transaction. Additionally, ERCU provides specialized Medicare guidance services through a licensed Medicare specialist (Deidra Broadbent), offering enrollment support and annual coverage reviews at no apparent additional cost to members.

As a community-chartered credit union, ERCU emphasizes personalized service with "hometown experience" and modern banking convenience. However, membership is geographically restricted to specific Tennessee counties or employment-based (for Adamsville residents). The credit union's website content is limited regarding asset size, full service details, and comprehensive product information, making it difficult to assess competitive positioning against larger institutions.
services: EMV chip debit cards with instant issue
Medicare specialist consultation and enrollment guidance
pros: Lifetime membership benefit—account remains active even after relocation, job changes, or retirement with minimal $5 balance requirement
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.99% during promotional periods
In-house real estate loan processing with rates from 5.50%
Free Medicare specialist services including enrollment guidance and annual coverage reviews
Instant-issue EMV chip card capability available at branches
Mobile app banking for account access and management on-the-go
Flexible payment options through RISE Payments system
cons: Membership eligibility restricted to specific Tennessee counties (Lawrence, Giles, Wayne, Henderson) or employer-based access in Adamsville
Limited digital information available on website regarding full product offerings, rates, and fees
No mention of surcharge-free ATM networks or shared branching capabilities typical of credit unions
Medicare services advertisement includes disclaimer that insurance products are not federally insured and credit union representation is limited to 4 organizations
Minimal details on savings account types, CD offerings, or deposit interest rates
best_for: Residents and employees in Lawrence, Giles, Wayne, Henderson, or McNairy counties seeking local credit union banking with lifetime membership stability
Individuals nearing retirement age interested in Medicare planning support combined with financial services
Auto and home loan borrowers wanting competitive rates through community-focused lender
Members prioritizing relationship-based banking and continued access regardless of employment or geographic changes
diagnosis: Employee Resources Credit Union is best for Tennessee residents within its service area seeking personalized, community-based credit union banking with the security of lifetime membership. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility that excludes those outside Lawrence, Giles, Wayne, Henderson, and McNairy counties (except Adamsville employment-based members), making it inaccessible to broader populations.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Employee Resources — NCUA-insured credit union in LAWRENCEBURG, TN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
